description:
You are a network engineer who just received a CSV export of the latest ping tests performed overnight.  
Your goal is to isolate every host that is completely unreachable (100 % packet loss) and publish a JSON
report that the NOC dashboard can ingest.

STARTING SET-UP  
1. The CSV file is already present at  
   /home/user/network/logs/ping_results.csv  

2. A writable directory already exists at  
   /home/user/network/reports  

WHAT YOU MUST DO  
A. Parse /home/user/network/logs/ping_results.csv and detect every row whose packet_loss column equals 100  
   (exact numeric value, no percent sign).  
B. Create a **single-line, minified JSON file** named  
   /home/user/network/reports/unreachable_summary.json  
   whose content follows this exact schema and ordering (all keys lowercase, no extra whitespace):  

   {"total_hosts_tested":<INTEGER>,"failed_hosts":[{"host":"<STRING>","reason":"packet_loss"}, … ]}

   • total_hosts_tested is the total number of data rows (excluding the header) found in the CSV.  
   • failed_hosts is an array containing one object per unreachable host, in the same order the hosts
     appear in the CSV.  
   • reason must always be the literal string “packet_loss” for each unreachable host.  

   Example of the precise formatting that will be verified (do **not** copy these values verbatim):  
   {"total_hosts_tested":3,"failed_hosts":[{"host":"router1.example.net","reason":"packet_loss"}]}  

C. Produce a plain-text log file named  
   /home/user/network/reports/generation.log  
   which must contain at least one line that includes the exact substring  
   SUMMARY CREATED  
   (capitalization must match).  You may include additional diagnostic text on the same line or on
   other lines, but the verification will look for that substring.

CONSTRAINTS  
• Solve the task with a simple sequence of 3–4 shell commands (you might combine awk/grep/cut/jq, etc.).  
• Do not modify the original CSV file.  
• Do not add extra whitespace or line breaks to the JSON output.  
• Ensure both files end up in /home/user/network/reports.  

VERIFICATION HINTS (what automated tests will check)  
1. /home/user/network/reports/unreachable_summary.json exists and is a single line.  
2. The JSON is syntactically valid, strictly minified, and matches the schema above.  
3. total_hosts_tested equals the number of data rows in ping_results.csv.  
4. failed_hosts contains exactly the hosts whose packet_loss value is 100.  
5. /home/user/network/reports/generation.log exists and contains the substring SUMMARY CREATED.  
6. No other files are altered.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/network/logs/ping_results.csv  
   Exact contents (including header line):  
   timestamp,host,latency_ms,packet_loss
   2023-07-01T10:00:00Z,host1.example.com,23,0
   2023-07-01T10:00:00Z,host2.example.com,,100
   2023-07-01T10:00:00Z,host3.example.com,45,0
   2023-07-01T10:00:00Z,host4.example.com,,100
   2023-07-01T10:00:00Z,host5.example.com,31,0

2. /home/user/network/reports  (empty directory, writable by the agent)

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/network/reports/unreachable_summary.json  
   Exact content (single line, no trailing newline required):  
   {"total_hosts_tested":5,"failed_hosts":[{"host":"host2.example.com","reason":"packet_loss"},{"host":"host4.example.com","reason":"packet_loss"}]}

2. /home/user/network/reports/generation.log  
   File exists and contains at least one line with the substring:  
   SUMMARY CREATED

Automated test suite will:
• verify the exact JSON string above (byte-for-byte match, no extra whitespace),  
• verify generation.log contains SUMMARY CREATED,  
• ensure no other files under /home/user/network/ have been modified.
